reasoning:
Without access to search tools in this environment, I must rely solely on base rates derived from historical stock market performance combined with seasonal context relative to the late October start date. 

The reference class is the probability of positive returns in the Dow Jones Industrial Average over intervals of 7 days, 30 days, and 90 days. Historically, the U.S. equity market drifts upward, with positive return base rates of roughly 54% over any given week, 60% over a 1-month period, and 64%-68% over a 3-month period. Additionally, momentum and seasonality factors come into play: late October traditionally marks the beginning of the strongest seasonal six-month window for U.S. equities (November through April). Thus, seasonal trends create a tailwind for these specific timeframes. I have adjusted the baseline probabilities slightly upward to account for the favorable November and December seasonality and the implicit bull-market momentum indicated by the Dow reaching over 46,000 as of mid-October 2025.
